zh.lifeSummary: 當William Hilton於1585年出生在英國爾諾維其時，他的父親William Hilton II35歲，母親unknown23歲。他於1616年與Mary Winslow在英國爾諾維其結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和2個女兒的父母。他於1590年住在英國爾維頓聖 倫恩斯 邱奇。他於1609年在英國米德萊可倫敦被列為fishmongers apprenticeship at london, england. 。他去世於1655年6月28日在美國緬因州約克約克享年70歲。

nameMeanings.name: Hilton
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: habitational name from any of various places called Hilton (e.g. in Cambridgeshire, Derbyshire, Staffordshire, North Yorkshire, and Westmorland), most of which derive from Old English hyll ‘hill’ + tūn ‘farmstead, estate’, though some come from Old English helde ‘slope’ + tūn.

occasionally, as in the case of Ralph filius Hilton (Yorkshire 1219), perhaps from the personal name Hilton, itself a possible form of Norman Hildun, composed of the ancient Germanic elements hild ‘strife, battle’ + hūn ‘bear cub’. Compare Hilt . This English surname is present in Ireland (mainly taken to Ulster in the early 17th century, though recorded earlier in Dublin).

variants.name: Hulton
meanings: English (Lancashire): habitational name from Hulton (Lancashire) or Hulton (Staffordshire), both from Old English hyll ‘hill’ + tūn ‘farmstead, estate’. Compare Hilton .

variants.name: Hilt
meanings: German: from a short form of Hildebrand or some other compound ancient Germanic name formed with hild ‘strife, battle’ as the first element. See Hild .
English (London): from the Middle English personal name Hilte, ultimately from ancient Germanic Helto, probably from halith- ‘hero’.

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

historicEvents.title: The First English Colony is Established in America
historicEvents.description: England established its first colony in North America in 1585 as a way to generate additional wealth. The colony was named Virginia, after the virgin Queen Elizabeth I.

historicEvents.title: Elizabeth I Signs Death Warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots
historicEvents.description: Elizabeth I signed the death warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots, on February 1, 1587, as she was found guilty of plotting to assasinate Elizabeth. Mary, Queen of Scots, was beheaded at Fotheringhay Castle in England on February 8, 1587.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Elizabeth I.org - Mary Queen of Scots

historicEvents.title: The Pilgrims Board the Mayflower
historicEvents.description: "The Pilgrims boarded the Mayflower in search of religious freedom in the New World in September 1620. The Mayflower set sail from Plymouth, England, with 102 passengers, 40 of whom called themselves ""Saints."" After two stormy months at sea, the ship reached the New World. Nearly half of the group died during their first winter due to malnutrition and the harsh New England winter."
Historical Boundaries: 1716: York, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America 1776: York, Massachusetts, United States 1820: York, Maine, United States

historicEvents.title: The English Civil War
historicEvents.description: A series of conflicts regarding England's governance during the years 1642 to 1651 is now known as The English Civil War. Charles I summoned supporters to join him against his enemies in Parliament. In October 1642, nearly 10,000 men fought for Charles I and chased Parliament across the River Tamar. Fighting continued for years and was finally ended at the Battle of Worcester on September 3, 1651, with a Parliamentarian victory.

en.lifeSummary: When William Hilton was born in 1585, in Northwich, Cheshire, England, his father, William Hilton II, was 35 and his mother, unknown, was 23. He married Mary Winslow in 1616, in Northwich, Cheshire, England. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 2 daughters. He lived in St Helen's Church, Witton, Cheshire, England in 1590. In 1609, his occupation is listed as fishmongers apprenticeship at london, england.  in London, Middlesex, England. He died on 28 June 1655, in York, York, Maine, United States, at the age of 70.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando William Hilton nasceu em 1585, em Northwich, Cheshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, William Hilton II, tinha 35 anos e sua mãe, unknown, tinha 23 anos. Ele casou-se com Mary Winslow em 1616, em Northwich, Cheshire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos e 2 filhas. Ele viveu em St Helen's Church, Witton, Cheshire, Inglaterra em 1590. Em 1609, sua profissão aparece como fishmongers apprenticeship at london, england.  em London, Middlesex, Inglaterra. Ele faleceu em 28 de junho de 1655, em York, York, Maine, Estados Unidos da América, com 70 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда William Hilton родился в 1585 г. в местности нортвич, чешир, Англия, его отцу по имени William Hilton II было 35 лет, а матери, которую звали unknown, 23. Он женился на женщине по имени Mary Winslow в 1616 г. в местности нортвич, чешир, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 2 дочери. Он жил в местности ст хелен'с чурч, виттон, чешир, Англия в 1590 году. В 1609 году он работал по профессии fishmongers apprenticeship at london, england.  в местности Лондон, миддлесекс, Англия. Он умер 28 июня 1655 г. в местности ерк, ерк, Мэн, Соединенные штаты в возрасте 70 лет.
